rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by tracing invalid identifiers or helper declarations and resolving root causes or removing invalid clauses.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when diagnostics reference subcomponent paths or implementation-specific features.
Avoid generating AGREE clauses that reference opaque Boolean data or perform numeric comparisons on non-scalar types without explicit requirement justification.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must only reference visible identifiers and features within the current scope; avoid introducing package-qualified or external references.
Implementation-level guarantees and assignments should be placed in component implementation annexes, not type annexes.
Ensure AGREE expressions use only valid data types and avoid unsafe type conversions or range comparisons on Boolean or opaque data.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and must be placed in appropriate annex scopes.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex to avoid unresolved identifier errors.
Boolean expressions in AGREE should only be used when the port is explicitly defined as an AGREE bool in scope.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes; move valid bindings to implementation annexes instead.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or opaque data into numeric scalars unless the requirement and AADL model explicitly support such usage.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
